priority_matrix.item: R01: Extract resolveId and resolveType helpers in lib/api.js
R02: Introduce Record.fromResponse() static factory method
R03: Rename _getFullPayload to toPayload and _getChangedPayload to toChangedPayload
R04: Promote _reset() to public reset() method on Record
R05: Extract SAML_ASSERTION_TYPE constant in lib/auth.js
R06: Replace let opts with const opts throughout lib/api.js and lib/auth.js
R08: Add runtime process.emitWarning deprecation to stream()
R09: Introduce endpoint-selection helpers on Connection prototype
R10: Replace executeOnRefresh flag argument with explicit _notifyAndResolve/_resolveOAuth methods
R11: Return new OAuth object instead of mutating caller's object in authenticate/refreshToken
R12: Introduce sobjectPath() path-builder helper in lib/api.js
R13: Evaluate and remove trivial getter/setter delegation in lib/auth.js
R14: Introduce typed RequestContext class to replace mutable opts bag
R15: Extract FAKE_CLIENT_ID constant in test/connection.js

implementation_sequence.rationale: Trivial, zero-risk constant extraction with no dependencies. Safe first commit.

implementation_sequence.item: R06: Replace let opts with const opts throughout
implementation_sequence.rationale: Mechanical style normalization with no semantic effect. Can be done in a single pass alongside R05.
Zero-risk comment cleanup. No code changes, no dependencies.

implementation_sequence.item: R08: Add runtime deprecation warning to stream()

implementation_sequence.rationale: Isolated to test file. No production code dependencies.
Must precede R02 (Record.fromResponse uses reset()) and R03 (coordinates the public API surface). Low risk rename.

implementation_sequence.item: R03: Rename _getFullPayload to toPayload and _getChangedPayload to toChangedPayload
implementation_sequence.rationale: Can run concurrently with R04. Establishes public payload API before R02 factory is added. Update lib/api.js callers in same commit.
Requires R04 (public reset()) to be in place. Eliminates the new Record() + reset() ceremony in three places in api.js.

implementation_sequence.item: R01: Extract resolveId and resolveType helpers
implementation_sequence.rationale: Eliminates inconsistency between sobject.getId() and util.findId() before further api.js work. Foundation for R14 cleanup.
Cleans up magic string duplication in api.js. Best applied after R01 since both touch the same functions.

implementation_sequence.item: R09: Introduce endpoint-selection helpers on Connection
implementation_sequence.rationale: Foundation for R10 and R11 — endpoint helpers must exist before the auth flow refactorings that use them.

implementation_sequence.item: R10: Replace executeOnRefresh flag with explicit intent
implementation_sequence.rationale: Apply together with R11 since both touch the same .then() blocks in authenticate() and refreshToken(). R09 must be complete first.

implementation_sequence.item: R11: Return new OAuth object instead of mutating caller's object
implementation_sequence.rationale: Applied in the same commit as R10 to avoid a state where authenticate() has the flag removed but still mutates. Coordinate with callers.

implementation_sequence.item: R13: Evaluate and remove trivial getter/setter delegation
implementation_sequence.rationale: After R10/R11 because those refactorings still use this.getUsername()/this.getPassword(). Removing getters last ensures no mid-refactoring breakage.
Highest-complexity change. Must follow R01 and R12 which clean up api.js. Apply one API function per commit with full test verification after each.
